name: Johns Hopkins Center
description: The 'Johns Hopkins Center' in conspiracy circles often refers to the Johns Hopkins Center for Health Security and its involvement in pandemic preparedness exercises, most notably 'Event 201,' a high-level simulation of a hypothetical coronavirus pandemic held in October 2019. Conspiracy theories allege that Event 201 was not merely a simulation but a deliberate plan or a 'script' for the COVID-19 pandemic, implying that Johns Hopkins and its partners (the World Economic Forum and the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation) either orchestrated the pandemic or had prior knowledge and facilitated its spread. Proponents point to the timing of the event shortly before the actual pandemic, the accuracy of some simulated outcomes, and the involvement of influential organizations as evidence. Mainstream perspectives dismiss these claims, emphasizing that pandemic preparedness exercises are crucial for developing effective response strategies. Academics and public health officials argue that Event 201 was a responsible effort to identify vulnerabilities and improve global pandemic readiness. They highlight that such simulations are standard practice in public health and that the accuracy of some simulated outcomes was due to the predictable nature of pandemics based on historical data and scientific understanding. Critics of the conspiracy theories also emphasize the lack of concrete evidence linking Event 201 to the actual origins or spread of COVID-19, which are attributed to natural zoonotic transmission.
subtitle: Pandemic simulations and conspiracy theories.

claims.claim: Event 201 was a deliberate plan for the COVID-19 pandemic.
claims.evidence: Timing of the event, accuracy of some simulated outcomes, involvement of influential organizations.
claims.criticism: Event 201 was a pandemic preparedness exercise; simulated outcomes were based on scientific understanding and historical data.

claims.claim: Johns Hopkins Center for Health Security orchestrated or facilitated the pandemic.
claims.evidence: Involvement in Event 201, alleged connections to global elites.
claims.criticism: No evidence supports this claim; the pandemic originated from natural zoonotic transmission.

claims.claim: Event 201 predicted the pandemic with uncanny accuracy, proving prior knowledge.
claims.evidence: Some simulated outcomes matched real-world events.
claims.criticism: Pandemic characteristics are predictable based on historical data and scientific models; some similarities are coincidental.

keyFigures.name: Bill Gates
keyFigures.role: Involved through the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation's partnership in Event 201 (often cited by proponents)

keyFigures.name: Tom Inglesby
keyFigures.role: Director of the Johns Hopkins Center for Health Security; publicly addressed concerns about Event 201

keyFigures.name: Eric Toner
keyFigures.role: Senior Scholar at the Johns Hopkins Center for Health Security; involved in the design of Event 201

timeline.event: Johns Hopkins Center for Health Security hosts Event 201.
First cases of COVID-19 reported in Wuhan, China.
Conspiracy theories linking Event 201 to the pandemic begin to circulate.
Debates continue regarding the origins of COVID-19 and the role of pandemic preparedness exercises.
